library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(arrow)
## Some features are not enabled in this build of Arrow. Run `arrow_info()` for more information.
## The repository you retrieved Arrow from did not include all of Arrow's features.
## You can install a fully-featured version by running:
## `install.packages('arrow', repos = 'https://apache.r-universe.dev')`.
## 
## Attaching package: 'arrow'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:lubridate':
## 
##     duration
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     timestamp
library(forecast)
library(tseries)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(knitr)
INPUT_FILE <- "../../data/processed/merged_features_weekly.parquet"
FREQ <- 52
ALPHA <- 0.05

calc_smape <- function(actual, pred) {
  mean(200 * abs(actual - pred) / (abs(actual) + abs(pred)), na.rm = TRUE)
}
df <- arrow::read_parquet(INPUT_FILE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  arrange(date)

if (!"target_was_missing" %in% names(df)) df$target_was_missing <- 0L

commodities <- unique(df$commodity)
all_results <- list()
all_models <- list()
all_preds <- list()

for (comm in commodities) {

  sub <- df %>%
    filter(commodity == comm) %>%
    arrange(date)

  train_df <- sub %>%
    filter(split == "train") %>%
    drop_na(target_price)

  test_df <- sub %>%
    filter(split == "test") %>%
    drop_na(target_price)

  if (nrow(train_df) < 30 || nrow(test_df) == 0) next

  n_test <- nrow(test_df)
  train_ts <- ts(train_df$target_price, frequency = FREQ)

  # Holt-Winters Additive
  model_add <- tryCatch(
    ets(train_ts, model = "AAA", damped = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  pred_add <- if (!is.null(model_add)) as.numeric(forecast(model_add, h = n_test)$mean) else rep(NA, n_test)

  # Holt-Winters Multiplicative
  model_mult <- tryCatch(
    {
      if (any(train_df$target_price <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) stop("Non-positive values")
      ets(train_ts, model = "MAM", damped = FALSE)
    },
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  pred_mult <- if (!is.null(model_mult)) as.numeric(forecast(model_mult, h = n_test)$mean) else rep(NA, n_test)

  # Automatic ETS
  model_ets <- tryCatch(
    ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  pred_ets <- if (!is.null(model_ets)) as.numeric(forecast(model_ets, h = n_test)$mean) else rep(NA, n_test)

  ets_label <- if (!is.null(model_ets)) paste0("ETS (", model_ets$method, ")") else "ETS (auto)"

  pred_df <- tibble(
    date = test_df$date,
    actual = test_df$target_price,
    was_missing = test_df$target_was_missing,
    additive = pred_add,
    multiplicative = pred_mult,
    ets_auto = pred_ets
  )

  real_idx <- pred_df$was_missing == 0

  smape_add <- if (!is.null(model_add))
    round(calc_smape(pred_df$actual[real_idx], pred_df$additive[real_idx]), 4)
  else NA

  smape_mult <- if (!is.null(model_mult))
    round(calc_smape(pred_df$actual[real_idx], pred_df$multiplicative[real_idx]), 4)
  else NA

  smape_ets <- if (!is.null(model_ets))
    round(calc_smape(pred_df$actual[real_idx], pred_df$ets_auto[real_idx]), 4)
  else NA

  models_fitted <- list(
    "HW Additive (AAA)" = model_add,
    "HW Multiplicative (MAM)" = model_mult
  )

  models_fitted[[ets_label]] <- model_ets

  results <- tibble(
    commodity = comm,
    model = c("HW Additive (AAA)", "HW Multiplicative (MAM)", ets_label),
    sMAPE = c(smape_add, smape_mult, smape_ets)
  ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(sMAPE))

  all_results[[comm]] <- results
  all_models[[comm]] <- models_fitted
  all_preds[[comm]] <- pred_df
}
## Warning in ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"): I can't handle data with frequency
## greater than 24. Seasonality will be ignored. Try stlf() if you need seasonal
## forecasts.
## Warning in ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"): I can't handle data with frequency
## greater than 24. Seasonality will be ignored. Try stlf() if you need seasonal
## forecasts.
## Warning in ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"): I can't handle data with frequency
## greater than 24. Seasonality will be ignored. Try stlf() if you need seasonal
## forecasts.
## Warning in ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"): I can't handle data with frequency
## greater than 24. Seasonality will be ignored. Try stlf() if you need seasonal
## forecasts.
results_df <- bind_rows(all_results)

ALL HOLT-WINTERS / ETS MODELS — sMAPE

commodity model sMAPE
Bulb Onions (Yellow) ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 8.7735
Cabbage ETS (ETS(M,Ad,N)) 14.0996
Carrots ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 13.0057
Lettuce ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 5.4449 
best_df <- results_df %>%
  group_by(commodity) %>%
  slice_min(sMAPE, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

BEST MODEL PER COMMODITY (by sMAPE)

commodity model sMAPE
Bulb Onions (Yellow) ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 8.7735
Cabbage ETS (ETS(M,Ad,N)) 14.0996
Carrots ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 13.0057
Lettuce ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 5.4449

Residual diagnostics for best models

## 
## #### Bulb Onions (Yellow) — ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 
## 
##   Ljung-Box(10) p-value: 0.1513 ✓
##   Shapiro-Wilk p-value: 0.0000 ✗
## 
## 
## #### Cabbage — ETS (ETS(M,Ad,N)) 
## 
##   Ljung-Box(10) p-value: 0.6816 ✓
##   Shapiro-Wilk p-value: 0.0000 ✗
## 
## 
## #### Carrots — ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 
## 
##   Ljung-Box(10) p-value: 0.3455 ✓
##   Shapiro-Wilk p-value: 0.0000 ✗
## 
## 
## #### Lettuce — ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 
## 
##   Ljung-Box(10) p-value: 0.2234 ✓
##   Shapiro-Wilk p-value: 0.0000 ✗
plots <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(best_df))) {

  comm <- best_df$commodity[i]
  best_model <- best_df$model[i]
  best_smape <- best_df$sMAPE[i]

  sub <- df %>%
    filter(commodity == comm) %>%
    arrange(date)

  train_df <- sub %>%
    filter(split == "train") %>%
    drop_na(target_price)

  cutoff <- max(train_df$date) - 365 * 2

  train_plot <- train_df %>%
    filter(date >= cutoff)

  pred_df <- all_preds[[comm]]

  pred_col <- switch(best_model,
    "HW Additive (AAA)" = "additive",
    "HW Multiplicative (MAM)" = "multiplicative",
    "ets_auto"
  )

  test_plot <- pred_df %>%
    select(date, actual, pred = all_of(pred_col))

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(
      data = train_plot,
      aes(x = date, y = target_price),
      color = "grey60",
      linewidth = 0.7
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = test_plot,
      aes(x = date, y = actual, color = "Actual"),
      linewidth = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = test_plot,
      aes(x = date, y = pred, color = "Forecast"),
      linewidth = 0.9,
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Actual" = "#2c3e50", "Forecast" = "#e74c3c"),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title = comm,
      subtitle = sprintf("%s  |  sMAPE = %.2f", best_model, best_smape),
      x = NULL,
      y = "Price"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )

  plots[[i]] <- p
}

if (length(plots) > 0) {

  combined <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2) +
    plot_annotation(
      title = "Holt-Winters / ETS — Best Model Forecast vs Actual",
      theme = theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
      )
    )

  print(combined)
}
